task_completion.description: The 'References' label is formatted as Heading 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'References' appears above the citations and is visibly larger and bolder than the body text, or the style selector in the Calligra Words toolbar displays 'Heading 1' when the cursor is on it.

task_completion.description: All 15 citations are in alphabetical order by the first author's last name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Scanning the first word of each citation paragraph shows a clear alphabetical progression (e.g., starting with A and ending with 'World Health Organization').

task_completion.description: All citations have hanging indents applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first line of every citation is flush with the left margin, while the second and subsequent lines of the same citation are visibly indented to the right.

task_completion.description: Journal names and book/report titles are italicized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Words matching the journal/report keywords (e.g., 'Pediatrics', 'Vaccine', 'Ten threats', 'PLOS ONE') are visibly slanted (italicized) within the citation text.

task_completion.description: The font for all citations is standardized to a 12pt serif font, matching the document body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The citations are in a uniform serif font (e.g., Times New Roman) and uniform size. There should be no monospaced text (Courier New) or visibly smaller (10pt) text remaining in the References section.

task_completion.description: The body text above the References section is unaltered and the document is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text above the references contains the required phrases (e.g., 'Vaccine hesitancy has been identified'). The Calligra Words window title bar does not show an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename), or there is a visible notification/action of saving.

integrity.description: The agent used Calligra Words to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Calligra Words graphical user interface being used to edit the .odt file, rather than a terminal text editor or a Python script modifying the file directly.

integrity.description: Hanging indents were applied using formatting tools rather than manual spacing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The indented lines in the citations align perfectly on the left. There are no jagged edges or visible space characters indicating the agent just pressed the spacebar multiple times to fake an indent.

integrity.description: The agent opened or referenced the APA guide.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/apa_references_guide.txt is seen open in a text editor or its contents are printed in the terminal during the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Document path: /home/ga/Documents/vaccine_hesitancy_brief.odt. Guide path: /home/ga/Desktop/apa_references_guide.txt. Journal/Report keywords to check for italicization: 'Pediatrics', 'PLOS ONE', 'Human Vaccines', 'Vaccine', 'Health Psychology', 'Mayo Clinic Proceedings', 'New England Journal', 'PLOS Currents', 'Royal Society', 'Ten threats'. Body text must contain: 'Vaccine hesitancy has been identified', 'Understanding the psychological', 'public health interventions must be tailored'. Note on alphabetical order: The 15th citation starts with 'World Health Organization', not just 'World'.
